page: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz, with a 105 MB L3 cache. The hardware includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with the Passmark CPU score, extended instructions, and encryption tests ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks also reaches the top 10%, while large block read and write operations perform in the top 25%. Single-core stress-ng performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the average category. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, which assist in cryptographic and vector-heavy workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high compute density and memory throughput relative to its resource profile, making it suitable for CPU-bound tasks without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. It is highly suited for workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), enhancing its efficiency for mathematical and cryptographic operations. This instance is well-suited for high-density compute workloads, including software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and language model prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 96 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.2 GHz
192 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and extended instructions
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and LLM prompt processing
